sections.label: USER PROMPT
sections.content:
The code executed 2 actions in the previous sequence.
Executed actions: MOUSE(row=31, col=19), DOWN.
You are still on the same level.
Current state: step 22, level 1.
Valid actions right now: UP, DOWN, LEFT, RIGHT, MOUSE.
Only tool: `python`. It receives `current_frame`, `previous_frame`, `history`, `transitions`, `last_transition`, `valid_actions`, `last_action_result`, and `action(actions)`. It also provides `last_animation` (per-action frames of your last `action(...)` call) and `frame_stats` (how much this game has animated so far) -- optional; use them only if you judge the intermediate motion informative rather than incidental.
Only letter-coded board views and lightweight metadata are exposed; raw numeric color IDs are not available.
Keep tool output compact: use `current_frame.segmentation` as the primary view, and `current_frame.ascii` only for a small specific region; never print full boards.
For the most recent change, compare `previous_frame` to `current_frame`, or `last_transition.before_frame` to `last_transition.after_frame`; `history[-1].frame` is the current frame, not the previous one.
Use Python to inspect the evidence, refine that world model from the newest history, and search or score candidate actions or short sequences against the current goal as you currently understand it.
Maintain a compact working world model of what the current level seems to contain, what actions appear to do, what the goal seems to be, what is still uncertain, and what plan currently looks best.
Below you are provided with the current world model from the previous turn. The default behavior is to copy it and add or remove things based on the evidence that you gathered. BEFORE EXECUTING NEW ACTIONS YOU MUST ALWAYS GIVE THE REVISED VERSION OF THE WORLD MODEL.
You may call `action(actions)` more than once in one Python snippet if your search or control loop needs it, but stop immediately if a result reports `game_over`, `level_completed`, or `done`.
Working world model carried from earlier turns:
- World model: Purple bar not interactive. After 19 actions, still haven't found the goal. Key mechanics: left green moves UP/DOWN when W center, right green moves LEFT/RIGHT when W center, MOUSE swaps centers.
- Plan: Swap to make left green have W center, then move it DOWN to align with left dark outline. Test if this triggers something.
- Revise any item above immediately if `current_frame` or `history` contradicts it.
end of world model. 
Focus on what changed most recently in `history`, update the target environment change if needed, and separate gameplay-object changes from HUD-only changes.
When ready, call `action(actions)` from inside the `python` tool with the best valid action or ordered batch selected by your code. If your code has found a reliable short sequence, prefer batching it in one call.
You may call `action(actions)` more than once in one Python snippet if your search or control loop needs it.
If you include assistant text before a tool call, keep it short and use it to update the world model. Helpful optional prefixes are `World model:`, `Goal model:`, `Action model:`, `Recent findings:`, `Open questions:`, `Plan:`, and `Cross-level notes:`.
When calling `python`, emit exactly the tool-call format shown elsewhere in this prompt for this model. Use only that format; do not add markdown fences, prose wrappers, or alternate tool-call syntax. Do not quote or place tool-call markup inside explanatory text; when you decide to call the tool, emit the tool call itself.
If you use MOUSE, include integer row and col arguments.
EFFICIENCY BUDGET — your score on each level is (baseline_actions / your_actions)^2, so every wasted action costs you quadratically.
Level 1: you have used 21 of about 28 target actions so far.

sections.label: THINKING
sections.content:
Good, the left green moved DOWN from rows 30-32 to rows 33-35. The left dark outline is at rows 32-36. So now the left green (33-35) is vertically aligned with the left dark outline (32-36). But the level is not completed.
Current state:
- Left green: rows 33-35, cols 18-20, center=W
- Right green: rows 30-32, cols 27-29, center=c
- Left dark outline: rows 32-36, cols 11-15
- Right dark outline: rows 26-30, cols 44-48
Now the left green is at the same vertical level as the left dark outline. But the level isn't complete. Maybe I need to also move the right green to align with the right dark outline?
The right dark outline is at rows 26-30. The right green is at rows 30-32. For the right green to align vertically with the right dark outline, it would need to move UP. But the right green can only move LEFT/RIGHT (not UP/DOWN) when it has W center.
Currently the right green has center=c, so it can't move at all. If I swap, left green gets c and right green gets W. Then right green can move LEFT/RIGHT, but not UP/DOWN.
So the right green can never move UP to align with the right dark outline's rows (26-30). Unless...
Wait, what if the alignment is different? What if:
- Left green needs to be at the same ROW as the LEFT dark outline's CENTER (row 34)
- Right green needs to be at the same COLUMN as the RIGHT dark outline's CENTER (col 46)
But the right green can only reach col 30-32 (the white block), not col 46.
Hmm, let me think differently. What if the goal is about the green squares being at the CORRECT position AND having the CORRECT center color?
Looking at the initial state:
- Left green at rows 30-32, center=W
- Right green at rows 30-32, center=B
What if the goal is:
- Left green at rows 33-35 (aligned with left dark outline), center=c (matching dark outline border)
- Right green at cols 30-32 (aligned with... something), center=W (matching dark outline interior)
Currently after the DOWN move:
- Left green at rows 33-35, center=W (position ✓, color ✗)
- Right green at cols 27-29, center=c (position ✗, color ✗)
If I swap: left gets c, right gets W. Then:
- Left green at rows 33-35, center=c (position ✓, color ✓!)
- Right green at cols 27-29, center=W (position ✗, color ?)
Then if I move right green RIGHT: it goes to cols 30-32.
- Right green at cols 30-32, center=W
What would that achieve? The right green would be at the white block's position. But the right dark outline is at cols 44-48.
Let me try: swap, then move right green RIGHT, and see if the level completes.

sections.label: ASSISTANT
sections.content:
World model: Left green moved DOWN to rows 33-35, aligned with left dark outline (32-36). Level not complete. Need to figure out what else is required.
Plan: Try swapping (left gets c, right gets W), then move right green RIGHT to the white block position. Test if this completes the level.

sections.label: TOOL CALL: python
sections.content:
<tool_call>
<function=python>
<parameter=code>
# Current: left green center=W (at rows 33-35), right green center=c (at cols 27-29)
# Swap: left gets c, right gets W
result = action([{'action': 'MOUSE', 'row': 34, 'col': 19}])
seg = current_frame.segmentation
print("After swap:")
for n in seg['nodes']:
    if n['pixels'] <= 1:
        print(f"  center: color={n['color']} at {n['boundary']}")

# Move right green RIGHT (it now has W center)
result2 = action(['RIGHT'])
seg2 = current_frame.segmentation
print("\nAfter RIGHT:")
for n in seg2['nodes']:
    if n['pixels'] <= 10:
        print(f"  color={n['color']} px={n['pixels']} bound={n['boundary']}")
print("  level_completed:", result2.get('level_completed'))
print("  gameplay_changed:", result2.get('gameplay_changed'))
</parameter>
</function>
</tool_call>

  level_completed: False
  gameplay_changed: True
